name: NMSLIB (Non-Metric Space Library for ANN)

performance.notes: C++ backend; HNSW implementation; competitive with faiss on CPU

agent_readiness.notes: Non-metric space approximate nearest neighbor library. import nmslib. index = nmslib.init(method='hnsw', space='cosinesimil'). index.addDataPointBatch(data). index.createIndex({'post': 2}, print_progress=True). ids, distances = index.knnQueryBatch(queries, k=10, num_threads=4). HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) algorithm. Supports non-Euclidean distance metrics including cosine, L1, L2, Jaccard. Founded hnswlib as a separate project. Now mostly superseded by hnswlib for HNSW.

gotchas: Less actively maintained than hnswlib or faiss
Verbose API compared to modern alternatives
Installation can be complex on some platforms
best_when: Reproducing older ANN benchmarks using NMSLIB
When hnswlib's Euclidean/cosine metrics are insufficient
avoid_when: Standard cosine/L2 similarity (hnswlib or faiss simpler)
New projects (use hnswlib, faiss, or usearch instead)
summary: NMSLIB is an approximate nearest neighbor library with HNSW implementation and support for non-metric distance spaces (Jaccard, custom). nmslib.init(method='hnsw', space='cosinesimil'); addDataPointBatch(); knnQueryBatch(). Historically important — spawned hnswlib as a standalone project. Now mostly superseded by hnswlib and faiss for standard use cases. Use for non-Euclidean/non-cosine distance metrics.
